title: Turn On Two-Factor Authentication
summary: Turn on an extra login security layer for an important account.
learnerPromise: You will be able to enable two-factor authentication on an important account and understand how to use it when logging in.
whyItMatters: Passwords can sometimes be guessed or stolen. Two-factor authentication adds a second step to verify it is really you, making your accounts much more secure.
realLifeUses: You want to protect your email account from being accessed by someone else
A service you use was part of a data breach and you want stronger protection
You keep important documents or photos in an online account you want to secure
You use online banking or payment apps and want an extra layer of safety
materialsNeeded: A phone or computer with internet access
Access to an email account or phone number for receiving verification codes
Optionally: an authenticator app such as Google Authenticator or your password manager
result: Two-factor authentication is enabled on at least one account and you have saved your backup codes.
youWillLearn: What two-factor authentication is and why it helps protect your accounts
How to find the security settings for an online account
How to choose a second verification method — text message, authenticator app, or security key
How to save backup codes in case you lose access to your second factor
miniChallenge: Enable two-factor authentication on one important account and save the backup codes in a safe place.
steps: Choose one important account to secure — such as email, cloud storage, or social media
Sign in and go to the account settings, then find the security or login section
Look for 'Two-Factor Authentication,' '2FA,' or 'Extra Security' and select it
Choose your second verification method — text message or authenticator app are most common
Follow the setup steps, test logging in with the new second step, and save your backup codes
completionCriteria: You successfully enabled two-factor authentication on one account
You saved the backup or recovery codes somewhere safe and separate from your device
commonProblems: The verification code does not arrive — check your phone signal or spam folder
Authenticator app codes do not work — make sure your device's clock is set to automatic time
Lost access to the second factor — use a saved backup code to regain access, then update your 2FA settings
The account does not offer two-factor authentication — consider adding a strong unique password instead
tips: Authenticator apps are generally more secure than text message codes
Save backup codes somewhere you can access without your phone — such as a written copy in a safe place
Consider using a password manager that also handles two-factor codes
Enable 2FA on your most important accounts first: email, banking, and cloud storage
